intended_use: Impala is intended to record, store, and transfer a 12-lead resting electrocardiogram (ECG). Impala acquires four standard diagnostic-bandwidth leads (Leads I, II, V2, V4, or Leads I, II, V1, V4). The
intended_use_full: Impala is intended to record, store, and transfer a 12-lead resting electrocardiogram (ECG). Impala acquires four standard diagnostic-bandwidth leads (Leads I, II, V2, V4, or Leads I, II, V1, V4). The device derives four standard diagnostic-bandwidth, Lead-III and unipolar limb leads aVR, aVF and aVL. The device also synthesizes Leads V1 or V2, V3, V5, V6, which are similar to but not identical to the same leads of a standard diagnostic 12-lead. The 4 synthesized chest leads are not intended for diagnostic use and may fail to show important findings limited to those leads.
target_condition: Resting electrocardiogram recording and ECG analysis for various cardiac conditions (arrhythmias, infarction, ischemia, blocks, hypertrophy, etc.)
ground_truth: Physician overread confirmed diagnosis with multiple diagnostic codes from Mayo Clinic ECG database; comparison to standard 10-electrode 12-lead ECG recordings
other_metrics: Results from bench performance testing on synthesized leads validation datasets indicated that synthesized leads tend to be similar to corresponding 12-leads but significant differences exist in many cases. Device optimized for PPV and specificity in Asymptomatic Mode.
